source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The setup for measuring the refractive index of a liquid by the limit method is shown in Figure. ABC is a right-angled prism. Its refractive index $n_g$ is known. A thin layer of the liquid to be measured is coated on its upper surface AB, and a piece of frosted glass (light above the glass) is covered on the liquid. Use an extended light source to illuminate in the direction of incidence, and the refracted angle of the light emitted from the AC surface of the prism will have a lower limit $i'$. If observed with a telescope, a semi-bright and semi-dark area with an obvious dividing line will appear in the field of view. Use $n_g$ and $i'$ from the problem to represent the refractive index $n$ of the liquid.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the setup is shown in a figure and refers to prism faces/points AB, AC, and ABC.
image_manifest contains 1 existing image with good reported readability_score=0.8428.
multi_step: Task asks to derive the liquid refractive index n from known prism refractive index n_g and observed lower-limit angle i'.
Problem involves combining the prism geometry in the figure with optics relations, not just reading off a value.
Answer is a formula expression rather than a single numeric value, suggesting a derivation with multiple linked steps.
choices has 4 formula options, indicating nontrivial reasoning/comparison among candidate expressions.
verifiability: answer_type=short_text, so the expected output is a constrained symbolic expression.
Multiple-choice options are provided, which can support downstream checking against a finite set of candidate formulas.
registry_passed=true, so core assets are present.
